title: Make structured inventory validation claims executable
goal: Implement #544 by making practical structured-file inventory schema claims executable.
Distinguish entries validated directly by the inventory checker from entries delegated to existing specialized checks.
Keep the improvement behind the existing structured inventory check rather than adding a new first-line command.
non_goals: Do not replace specialized cross-file validators with JSON Schema.
Do not validate ignored local caches by default.
Do not add a new top-level command or startup/report payload.
Do not make ordinary startup heavier.

intent.outcome: structured-file inventory schema_or_validator claims are executable or explicitly delegated/descriptive
intent.constraints: repo/tool/language agnostic; no new first-line surfaces; use existing inventory and contract checks
intent.latitude: may add narrow inventory metadata, checker helpers, tests, and inventory entry updates
intent.escalation: stop if broad validator semantics would duplicate specialized package or contract checks

execution.next_step: archive #544, close #544 and #502, then refresh evidence and continue the next prioritized lane

invariants: existing check_structured_file_inventory.py remains the front door
schema-backed direct validation is limited to feasible local JSON schema paths or JSON Schema draft markers
typed-validator-backed entries point to executable checks or explicitly delegated validators
startup and default report output stay unchanged

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: all machine-readable files are schema-backed, typed-validator-backed, generated/derived, or explicitly classified diagnostics
intent_continuity.this slice completes the larger intended: yes if no remaining inventory claims are merely ambiguous prose

required_continuation.activation trigger: none for #502 after #544 proof unless a future structured file appears without classification or executable/delegated validation

intent_interpretation.literal request: Continue backlog lane-by-lane while dogfooding and creating immediate improvement issues when useful.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: make the structured-file inventory trustworthy without adding product residue
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: inventory enforcement metadata plus direct/delegated validation in the existing checker
intent_interpretation.review guidance: verify that simple schema claims are checked and delegated claims identify executable checks

delegated_judgment.hard constraints: No new first-line command, no default startup/report expansion, no replacement of specialized validators.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: metadata shape, direct validation helper names, which schema claims are feasible to validate directly, and focused tests.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A claim cannot be made executable or delegated without changing unrelated validator architecture.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: structured inventory checker/schema/tests, structured_file_inventory.json, active plan/state
execution_bounds.max changed files: about 6 unless proof reveals a narrow additional owner
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run pytest tests/test_structured_file_inventory.py -q; uv run python scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py --quiet-success; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py --quiet-success; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py --strict; uv run ruff check scripts/check tests src/agentic_workspace/contracts
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: before changing broad contract-tooling architecture or adding a new command
execution_bounds.stop before touching: startup/report defaults, package payload semantics, ignored local cache validation

stop_conditions.stop when: direct schema validation creates false authority over specialized cross-file checks
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: inventory needs a new command or runtime-specific integration to satisfy the issue
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: implementation spreads into generated adapters or package lifecycle behavior
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: existing checker cannot distinguish direct and delegated claims without a larger metadata migration

active_milestone.scope: structured inventory claim enforcement metadata, checker logic, and tests

immediate_next_action: Add direct/delegated validation semantics to the structured inventory checker and tests.
invariants: inventory remains a validation/check surface, not startup doctrine
simple schema claims become executable where practical
validation_commands: uv run ruff check scripts/check tests src/agentic_workspace/contracts
completion_criteria: Simple schema-backed inventory entries validate matched JSON files against the named local schema where feasible.
Inventory entries can explicitly state direct, delegated, draft-schema, or descriptive enforcement.
Typed-validator-backed entries point to executable validators/checks or are explicitly classified as delegated/descriptive.
Focused tests fail on unsupported unverifiable claims and pass for current records.

execution_run.what happened: Added executable claim validation to the existing structured-file inventory checker: schema-backed JSON entries with local .schema.json claims now validate matched files; draft-schema claims validate matched schemas; typed-validator-backed claims must name an executable/delegated validator, checker, parser, doctor, or runtime. Added focused tests for valid direct schema claims, invalid matched JSON, missing schemas, invalid draft schemas, and unsupported typed-validator prose.
execution_run.scope touched: structured inventory checker/tests plus active planning state and execplan
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest tests/test_structured_file_inventory.py -q; uv run pytest tests/test_structured_file_inventory.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q; uv run python scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py --quiet-success; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py --quiet-success; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py --strict; uv run ruff check scripts/check tests src/agentic_workspace/contracts; uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/contracts scripts/check tests/test_structured_file_inventory.py; uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed <slice paths> --format json; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json; dogfooding closeout report/reconcile/skills commands
execution_run.result for continuation: #544 is complete and satisfies the remaining #502 structured-file-contract lane unless future refreshed evidence identifies another structured-file gap.
execution_run.next step: archive this plan, mark #502/#544 complete in state, commit/push, close issues, refresh external evidence, and continue the next prioritized lane

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; no new command or startup/report payload was added
finished_run_review.intent served: yes; practical schema-backed inventory claims are executable and typed-validator-backed claims must be executable/delegated rather than arbitrary prose
finished_run_review.config compliance: used start/summary/proof/doctor/report/reconcile/skills surfaces and kept the change inside existing checks
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low; specialized validators remain delegated and ignored local caches are not validated by default

proof_report.proof achieved now: focused tests, inventory check, contract tooling check, planning-surface check, ruff, proof-selected commands, and planning doctor passed
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: 23 structured inventory tests and 56 structured inventory/contract tooling tests passed; check_structured_file_inventory.py validates current tracked records; proof selected planning_surfaces and contract_tooling lanes and their commands passed

intent_satisfaction.original intent: #544: make structured inventory schema/validator claims executable or explicitly delegated.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: #544 added executable schema/draft validation and delegated typed-validator checks to the existing structured inventory checker; focused tests and proof-selected checks passed.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Make the package streamlined, lightweight, frictionless, cheap, modular, and small while preserving leverage.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: strengthen an existing check rather than adding a command or startup surface
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does this make inventory trust cheaper without increasing first-contact burden?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #544 closes the remaining known #502 structured-file inventory validation gap without adding a new command or startup surface.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Structured inventory tests, inventory check, contract tooling check, planning-surface check, ruff, proof-selected commands, and planning doctor passed.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen if tracked structured files can claim schema-backed or typed-validator-backed status without executable validation or explicit delegation.

durable_residue.learned constraint: inventory trust is cheapest when executable validation lives in the existing inventory check rather than a new command or prose doctrine
durable_residue.motivation worth preserving: agents can rely on inventory classifications without manually reconstructing whether schema_or_validator claims are real
durable_residue.promotion trigger: promote to docs only if maintainers need human-facing guidance for adding new inventory entries

execution_summary.follow-on routed to: none for #502; continue next refreshed lane
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: plan closeout records package-use effects and no-new-issue decision
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): none; checker/tests are the durable residue
